main_controller: ESP32-WROOM-32U development board with external antenna connector
scope: Build the complete base system first; keep Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W and HackRF One as a later optional SDR workspace
module_fidelity: Start with generic educational modules; replace them with exact variants after product links or photographs are supplied
physical_construction: Modular boards mounted on an acrylic or metal baseplate
placement: Drag, fixed/fine rotation, height, duplicate, lock, group, align, distribute, grid snap, mounting-hole snap, collision checks, antenna keep-outs, cable-bend clearance and mounting alignment; no automatic layout optimisation in the first release
simulation: Levels 1–3: visual assembly, electrical validation and interactive mock behaviour; no firmware/MCU emulation
rf_simulation: User-adjustable mock activity, animated signal paths and JSON/CSV imports; keep an interface boundary for future live serial/WebSocket data
cables: Typed cable library with realistic diameter, connector ends, minimum bend radius, material and colour rules
wire_editing: Manual control points, editable wire sections, harness bundles, labels, length display and intersection warnings; no automatic obstacle routing initially
one_click: A complete-circuit preset and a guided-build wizard inside the standalone 3D application
layouts: Functional-zone, bus-oriented and signal-flow presets; compact enclosure layout deferred
antennas: Model antenna hardware, orientation, keep-out zones, separation guidance and enclosure/metal warnings
assets: Hybrid: procedural geometry for boards, headers, terminals and simple parts; optimised GLB for complex connectors, displays, antennas and major modules
performance: Balanced realism with Low, Medium and High quality modes
verification_modes: Documented conceptual circuit mode plus a later validated-hardware mode
power: USB power bank input with fuse, reverse-polarity protection, main switch and power LED; protected 5 V distribution and low-noise 3.3 V RF rail
delivery: Markdown prompt folder first; GitHub issues only after approval
git: Separate feature branch per prompt; merge into an integration branch only after tests pass; do not merge directly to main
